key_insight: HPG-Diff combines diffusion generation with physics-guided feature alignment and a differentiable connectivity penalty to produce more physically consistent topologies.
engineering_relevance: It improves topology optimization generalization to unseen boundary conditions while reducing floating-material artifacts that can compromise manufacturability.
method: Hierarchically inject precomputed physics features into the denoising steps to steer designs toward valid load paths.
Add a virtual heat-propagation-based floating material suppression loss as a differentiable connectivity constraint.
Use lightweight LoRA fine-tuning to adapt the model to rectangular non-square domains with limited data.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports average compliance errors of 0.87% in-distribution and 5.29% out-of-distribution, with floating material ratios reduced to 2.90% and 2.44%, respectively.
watch_item: The connectivity and transfer results are promising, but the LoRA adaptation to non-square domains is only supported by preliminary case studies and may require careful validation.
paper_title: HPG-Diff: Hierarchical physics-guided diffusion with differentiable connectivity constraints for topology optimization
